title: Investorer: Flere opjusteringer venter i Novo
summary_md:
# Investorer: Flere opjusteringer venter i Novo

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-05-06  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode centers on Novo Nordisk's Q1 report, which beat expectations and triggered a small guidance upgrade and a ~7% stock pop; the hosts frame Novo as a cheap value stock with multiple further upgrades likely in 2026 as oral GLP-1 (Wegovy pill) momentum builds. Other Danish reporting-day movers dominate: hearing-aid maker Demant (the day's big winner, ~16%), Vestas (strong beat but stock fell on short-driven/algo trading), Netcompany (AI as opportunity), DSV (Amazon freight-platform threat plus May 12 capital markets day), and ALK-Abelló (raised guidance on allergy tablet strength). Overall mood is cautiously optimistic — Danish companies guided conservatively early in the year and are now positioned to beat.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Cheap value stock at PE ~12-14 vs Lilly ~28, ~5% dividend yield; oral pill (Wegovy) sales beat (>2bn vs ~1bn expected); conservative guidance with multiple upgrades likely.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see the small upgrade as a turning point removing downside-surprise risk. Oral GLP-1 captures new patients (no cannibalization, 2 of 3 new patients), GLP-1 market growing 30%/yr, Medicare entry in H2 2026 and September capital markets day as catalysts. Big upside if estimates start turning up given depressed valuation; preferred over Eli Lilly on valuation vs growth.

#### Sell Triggers

- if oral pill volume/total-market expansion disappoints
- if US price cuts hit harder than volume gains
- if 2026 revenue lands at bottom of -12% range
- if pipeline update at September CMD underwhelms

> *"Nej, jag vill egentligen köpa den. För som den är vurderad nu här så är det ju väsentligt bättre än en kontantinskud. Det är ju en value-aktie."*

---

### 🟢 DEMANT — Demant | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Day's biggest winner ~16%; strong Q1 (+6% vs +3.8% expected, DKK6.3bn rev), AI hearing aid with great battery, less likely to hit low end of guidance, upgrade signaled.

#### Reasoning

Johnny bought in Feb/March calling it too cheap; conference call signaled conservatism with strong Q1 start. Structural growth case (ageing population, ~6% historic industry growth), gaining market share (~10% growth), good capital allocation, DKK0.5bn cost program. Could be re-rated as a growth stock again with multiple expansion. Main risk is US consumer caution.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Middle East conflict persists and keeps oil/consumer sentiment weak
- if US consumers pull back further
- if new product Sihl fails to gain traction
- if no upgrade materializes as hinted

> *"Markant höjt upp härifrån ... jag kan inte se varför den inte skulle ligga markant högre om det inte kommer något alltså uförsett"*

---

### 🟢 DSV — DSV | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Down to 1483 from >1900, hit ~10% on AI fears then ~10% on Amazon freight-platform threat; world's largest logistics firm, best-in-class earner; May 12 capital markets day catalyst.

#### Reasoning

Johnny not nervous; sees stock as heavily oversold. Amazon competition may accelerate consolidation DSV can exploit by acquiring more firms at attractive valuations; DSV strong in complex solutions Amazon likely avoids. Big DB Schenker integration + AI synergies (billions DKK) expected to be announced May 12, potentially a positive catalyst/re-rating. Underlying business strong; Middle East war delaying an upgrade.

#### Sell Triggers

- if May 12 CMD synergy guidance disappoints
- if Amazon takes meaningful market share in complex logistics
- if Middle East conflict keeps freight customers cautious
- if global freight volumes deteriorate

> *"jag är slet inte nervös för DSV och jag tycker också att den har blivit kraftigt misshandlad på det senaste"*

---

### 🟢 VWS — Vestas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Strong beat (EBIT DKK127m vs 71m exp), order book 5.2bn vs 4.8bn, new EUR100m buyback, but stock fell on missed service contracts and heavy short positioning; turnaround on track.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see the beat as confirmation the turnaround works; best portfolio stock over the past year. CEO targets >10% EBIT margin plus revenue growth, so margin lift plus re-rating ('2+2=8'). Energy security tailwind (away from Russian/US LNG). Short-term contract slips and quant/retail-driven volatility are noise; focus on long-term trajectory. Ole expected it above 200 today.

#### Sell Triggers

- if EBIT margin target of >10% slips
- if service-revenue declines persist beyond contract timing
- if order intake weakens materially
- if structural turnaround stalls

> *"den var den bästa aktien för oss sista året ... Han är på väg och mår och levererar, det är deras målsättning, plus 10 procent i EBIT-marknaden"*

---

### 🟢 NETC — Netcompany | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Sales +16%, opened ~8% down then recovered to flat/positive; AI/agentic AI a driver for regulated, critical sectors; portfolio +20% since entry; heavily shorted (~6%).

#### Reasoning

CEO Rokoszewski pushes AI hard into the investment case; agentic AI changes software delivery in regulated/critical environments (defense, energy, banking) where tested, accountable European vendors are needed — an opportunity, not a threat, like DSV. European positioning vs US-heavy Palantir is an edge; took full ownership of Copenhagen Airport contract. Both hosts more positive post-report.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI tools commoditize Netcompany's products
- if existing-business growth slows
- if guidance/Q1 call (11:00) disappoints
- if short pressure reflects deteriorating fundamentals

> *"för mig att se tillsammans med DSV, kunskapsintelligencen det är en möjlighet, inte en trussel för Netcompany"*

---

### 🟢 ALK-B — ALK-Abelló | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Raised guidance on strong allergy-tablet sales; EBIT margin now to exceed 25%; promising peanut allergy study (half of 150 patients cured within 24h); Ole's 2028 target DKK325, ~12%/yr return.

#### Reasoning

Ole sees consistent 'ticking the box' execution since the capital markets day ~2 years ago; tablet business driving higher gross/EBIT margins. Peanut allergy data extends the runway in a large, serious-disease market. Held since 2010 as a long-term winner; potential C25 index inclusion (market cap ~DKK60bn) could add upside, hampered only by Lundbeck Foundation's 70% stake limiting liquidity.

#### Sell Triggers

- if clinical trials (e.g. food allergy) fail
- if genetic/ethnic subgroups don't respond to therapy
- if EBIT margin fails to exceed 25%
- if tablet sales growth decelerates

> *"jag har uppdaterat mål 2028 som nyligen var 325 kronor per aktie och det ger cirka 12% avkast i året så den behåller jag bara"*

---

### 🟡 LLY — Eli Lilly | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Superior growth (26-31% sales, >50% EPS) and best product before Novo's high-dose launch, but trades expensive at PE ~28; hosts doubt growth durability and expect Novo to win the pill market.

#### Reasoning

Acknowledged as the quality grower with the best weight-loss product, but Ole argues the growth rate isn't sustainable and normalizes, leaving Lilly at ~28x vs Novo at ~13x — nearly double the price. Hosts expect Novo's oral pill to beat Lilly's (which must be taken on empty stomach, more side effects, lower weight loss). Preference clearly tilts to Novo on valuation-vs-growth.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth rate proves not sustainable and normalizes
- if Novo's oral pill outcompetes on weight loss
- if valuation premium compresses toward Novo's

> *"den här växträtten du nämnde, den tror jag inte är vedvarande för Lillis vidkommande så det normaliseras"*

---
